judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent grounding; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: Group A — `pipeline/registry.py` (F-02, F-05, F-09):** three independent one-line/one-entry config edits ... One engineer, one PR

report_2.evidence: Engineer C: F-02 + F-05 + F-09, all in `pipeline/registry.py` — bundled to one owner because they're three edits to the same 12-line file; splitting them across engineers would produce three near-simultaneous diffs to adjacent lines for no benefit.

report_1.evidence: Group C — `pipeline/rubric.py` + tests (F-01, F-11):** one engineer ... Start second, in parallel with Group B ... with the two test-only findings (F-11, F-12) sequenced *after* their respective fixes land

report_2.evidence: Engineer C: F-02 + F-05 + F-09, all in `pipeline/registry.py` — bundled to one owner ... Engineer D: F-01 (`pipeline/rubric.py`) plus F-11 (new rubric-parsing edge-case test ...)

report_1.evidence: F-06 needs a threshold decision before it's actionable as an engineering task. ... it should go back to whoever owns the rubric/grading criteria for specific target values first.

report_2.evidence: F-06 asks to "tighten" `PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS` in `pipeline/score.py` — this changes the score every saved answer in every dataset receives, not just a code defect ... needs an explicit decision (specific new threshold values, and sign-off from whoever owns the scoring rubric)

report_1.evidence: `tests/conftest.py` defaults `RESULTS_DB` to a fixed path, `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`. If multiple engineers run the test suite concurrently on a shared machine/CI runner without overriding `RESULTS_DB`, they can read/write the same file and produce flaky or corrupted results ... An explicit override for `RESULTS_DB` (e.g. a per-engineer temp path)

report_2.evidence: `tests/conftest.py` defines `RESULTS_DB = os.environ.get("RESULTS_DB", "/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db")` — a single real filesystem path. If engineers ... run their suites concurrently on the same machine/CI without overriding `RESULTS_DB` ... parallel test runs could read/write the same file and produce flaky or corrupted results
